commit.message:
8384941: AArch64: Use stlr for standalone release stores

On AArch64, putOrdered* / putXRelease / setRelease currently lower in
C2 to a leading "dmb ish" followed by a plain "str". The release
ordering can be provided by "stlr" alone, making the dmb redundant.

Recognize this pattern during AArch64 instruction selection and emit
a single "stlr" instead:

  - needs_releasing_store() also returns true for is_release() stores
    without a trailing membar (i.e. setRelease / putOrdered, but not
    volatile, which is already covered), upgrading their emission from
    "str" to "stlr".

  - unnecessary_release() walks the leading MemBarRelease's memory
    chain (passing transparently through MergeMem and MemBarCPUOrder)
    and, if it drives a release store with no trailing membar, elides
    the dmb. Constructor final-field publication is left untouched
    because its preceding stores are MO=unordered and so do not match.

A new product flag UseStlrForStandaloneRelease (default false, opt-in) gates
both halves so they stay consistent. The transform is contained in
src/hotspot/cpu/aarch64/; no shared C2 changes are required since the
pattern is fully identifiable from the existing IR (MemBarRelease
standalone kind, StoreNode is_release / trailing_membar).

Tests:
  - test/hotspot/jtreg/compiler/c2/aarch64/TestStlrForStandaloneRelease.java
    Drives the three API surfaces (sun.misc.Unsafe.putOrderedX,
    jdk.internal.misc.Unsafe.putXRelease, VarHandle.setRelease) over
    int / long / ref fields and int[] / Object[] array elements, and
    asserts on PrintOptoAssembly output that stlr is emitted (and dmb
    elided) when the flag is on, and that the legacy str + dmb pattern
    remains when the flag is off.

  - test/micro/org/openjdk/bench/vm/compiler/StlrForStandaloneRelease.java
    JMH microbench covering the same three API surfaces over fields,
    array elements (single- and varying-index), each forked with both
    flag values.

Co-authored-by: xiejf2020 <xiejf2020@outlook.com>
